# Chart-Type Taxonomy (auto-generated)

**Do not edit this file by hand.** Edit `shared/taxonomies/chart_types.py`,
then run `python3 -m shared.taxonomies.chart_types --emit-md > stages/stage-1.5-evidence-execution/chart_types.md`.

## Trivial chart types

A figure plan composed entirely of these chart types fails Stage 1B as
chart-type-shortcut risk.

- `bar chart`
- `boxplot`
- `heatmap`
- `histogram`
- `horizontal bar chart`
- `line chart`
- `scatter plot`
- `scatter plot + regression line`
- `violin plot`

## Non-trivial chart types

At least one figure in any non-exempt empirical/computational plan
must come from this set.

- `alluvial diagram`
- `chord diagram`
- `confusion matrix grid`
- `decomposition`
- `decomposition panel`
- `force-directed layout`
- `graph with partition overlay`
- `multi-panel composition`
- `network graph`
- `parallel coordinates`
- `partition overlay`
- `ridge plot`
- `small multiples`
- `treemap`

## Network topic terms

If `study_spec.json` mentions any of these terms and the
rendered figures use no `NETWORK_CHART_TERMS` chart type, the
detector fails.

- `centrality`
- `community`
- `edge`
- `graph`
- `leiden`
- `louvain`
- `modularity`
- `network`
- `node`
- `partition`

## Network chart terms

- `force-directed`
- `graph with partition`
- `network graph`
- `node link`
- `node-link`
- `partition overlay`
